perturbation.original_text: “Proprietary Information” means any and all information and material disclosed by Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives in connection with the Proposed Transaction or in the course of the parties’ evaluation and negotiation of the Proposed Transaction, together with all communications, data, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, records, notes, lists, financial statements or other materials or information prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that contain or otherwise reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party or that reflect the review of, interest in, or evaluation of all or any portion of the Proposed Transaction or Disclosing Party’s business (collectively, “ Derived Information ”), whether tangible or intangible, furnished or prepared in writing or in oral, graphic, electronic or any other form or manner.
perturbation.changed_text: “Proprietary Information” means any and all information and material disclosed by Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives in connection with the Proposed Transaction or in the course of the parties’ evaluation and negotiation of the Proposed Transaction, together with all communications, data, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, records, notes, lists, financial statements or other materials or information prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that contain or otherwise reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party or that reflect the review of, interest in, or evaluation of all or any portion of the Proposed Transaction or Disclosing Party’s business (collectively, “ Derived Information ”), whether tangible or intangible, furnished or prepared in writing or in oral form or manner.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Proprietary Information' as information furnished in writing, orally, graphically, electronically, or in any other form. The modified text removes 'graphic, electronic or any other,' restricting the forms Proprietary Information can take. This contradicts later sections that assume Proprietary Information can be transmitted electronically, for example, Section 8 which discusses returning Proprietary Information on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files. This creates uncertainty in enforcement regarding electronically transmitted data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF MATERIALS. If either party hereto shall determine that it does not wish to proceed with the Proposed Transaction, such party shall promptly advise the other party of that decision. In that case, or if the Proposed Transaction otherwise is not consummated for any reason, Receiving Party shall, upon Disclosing Party’s written request, promptly deliver to Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information, and, at Disclosing Party’s sole election, return or destroy (provided that my such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of Receiving Party) all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) in Receiving Party’s or any of its Representatives’ possession; provided , that if a legal proceeding has been instituted to seek disclosure of the Proprietary Information, such material shall not be destroyed until the proceeding is settled or a final judgment with respect thereto has been rendered. However, Receiving Party may retain in the office of its legal counsel, one copy of Proprietary Information for record purposes only. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of any Proprietary Information, or documents or material containing or reflecting any Proprietary Information, the parties will continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for the term of this Agreement (or such other term as may be applicable to the specific obligation), except as otherwise specifically provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Proprietary Information' by removing 'graphic, electronic or any other' forms of information, restricting the types of proprietary information. This directly contradicts the 'RETURN OF MATERIALS' section, which explicitly mentions returning Proprietary Information on 'intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files'. The modified text now excludes electronic forms, creating a clear inconsistency in what constitutes Proprietary Information and what must be returned.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall take the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential proprietary information of similar nature and importance (but in no event less than reasonable care) to protect the confidentiality and avoid the use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall take the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential proprietary information of similar nature and importance (but in no event greater than minimal care) to protect the confidentiality and avoid the use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text sets a minimum standard of 'reasonable care'. The modified text sets a maximum standard of 'minimal care.' This contradicts Section 7, 'REMEDIES,' which implies a higher standard of care is expected by allowing Disclosing Party to seek an injunction for unauthorized disclosure. If minimal care is deemed sufficient, seeking an injunction becomes contradictory, as it implies a higher standard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REMEDIES. Receiving Party agrees that, due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, the unauthorized disclosure or use of the Proprietary Information may cause injury to Disclosing Party, the extent of which will be difficult to ascertain and for which there may be no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, Receiving Party agrees that Disclosing Company, in addition to any other available remedies, may have the right to seek an immediate injunction and other equitable relief enjoining any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement ordered by the court. Receiving Party shall notify Disclosing Party immediately if Receiving Party has reason to believe that any person who has had access to the Proprietary Information (including Receiving Party or any of its Representatives) has violated or intends to violate the terms of this Agreement or otherwise disclose any Proprietary Information in violation of the terms hereof. Any and all remedies herein expressly conferred upon a party will be deemed cumulative with and not exclusive of any other remedy conferred hereby, or by law or equity upon such party, and the exercise by a party of any one remedy will not preclude the exercise of any ether remedy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the minimum standard of 'reasonable care' with a maximum standard of 'minimal care'. This directly contradicts Section 7, 'REMEDIES,' which allows the Disclosing Party to seek an injunction for unauthorized disclosure. The original text sets a floor for the degree of care, whereas the changed text creates a ceiling. The contradicted text assumes a standard of care that, if breached, would warrant injunctive relief. By setting a 'minimal care' standard, the change makes it unclear when injunctive relief would be appropriate, creating an in-text contradiction because minimal care implies a lower standard than is assumed by the remedies section.

perturbation.original_text: Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction.
perturbation.changed_text: Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that the no-solicitation agreement applies to key personnel 'that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction.' The modified clause removes that limitation, making the no-solicitation agreement much broader. This in-text contradicts with the 'provided' clause, stating that general solicitations are allowed, potentially drawing key technical personnel. Removing the clause makes it unclear if key personnel are allowed to be generally solicited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Solicitation of Employees . Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction. Additionally, the above restriction shall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party from making offers of employment or offering retention packages to any employees of Company or any of its affiliates in connection with the Proposed Transaction, provided that such offers have been disclosed to the Board of Directors of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the no-solicitation agreement to key personnel introduced by the company or involved in the transaction discussions. The changed text broadens this to include *any* key technical or management personnel. This broader scope directly contradicts the 'provided' clause in the Contradicted Text, as it is now unclear whether general solicitations are permitted for *any* key personnel, including those not originally involved in the transaction discussions. This represents a strong inconsistency, as it is unclear whether a general solicitation that attracts key technical or management personnel of the Company violates the no-solicitation provision, due to the removal of the limitation. Therefore it creates an inconsistency and thus an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party expressly confirms and agrees that, as of the date hereof, it is not required to make any public disclosure with respect to (a) the Proposed Transaction (or the terms or conditions of any other acts relating thereto), (b) any item of Proprietary Information (or the fact that Proprietary Information bas been made available to such party), or (c) any discussions or negotiations taking place between the parties with respect to the Proposed Transaction, whether pursuant to the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”), the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder, or similar requirements related to general disclosure. If, after the date of this Agreement either party determines that any such disclosure is required, no such disclosure shall be made unless and until such party consults with the other party regarding the necessity and form of any such disclosure, and provides the other party a reasonable opportunity to review the proposed disclosure and comment thereon.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party expressly confirms and agrees that, as of the date hereof, it is required to make public disclosure with respect to (a) the Proposed Transaction (or the terms or conditions of any other acts relating thereto), (b) any item of Proprietary Information (or the fact that Proprietary Information bas been made available to such party), or (c) any discussions or negotiations taking place between the parties with respect to the Proposed Transaction, whether pursuant to the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”), the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder, or similar requirements related to general disclosure. If, after the date of this Agreement either party determines that any such disclosure is required, no such disclosure shall be made unless and until such party consults with the other party regarding the necessity and form of any such disclosure, and provides the other party a reasonable opportunity to review the proposed disclosure and comment thereon.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states each party 'is not required' to make public disclosures. The modified text states each party 'is required' to make public disclosures. The subsequent sentence, 'If, after the date of this Agreement either party determines that any such disclosure is required...' directly contradicts the first sentence of the modified text. It implies that disclosure is not always required, while the first sentence states it is always required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Public Disclosure Required . Each party expressly confirms and agrees that, as of the date hereof, it is required to make public disclosure with respect to (a) the Proposed Transaction (or the terms or conditions of any other acts relating thereto), (b) any item of Proprietary Information (or the fact that Proprietary Information bas been made available to such party), or (c) any discussions or negotiations taking place between the parties with respect to the Proposed Transaction, whether pursuant to the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”), the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder, or similar requirements related to general disclosure. If, after the date of this Agreement either party determines that any such disclosure is required, no such disclosure shall be made unless and until such party consults with the other party regarding the necessity and form of any such disclosure, and provides the other party a reasonable opportunity to review the proposed disclosure and comment thereon.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'is not required' with 'is required', directly contradicting the subsequent sentence 'If, after the date of this Agreement either party determines that any such disclosure is required...'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the first sentence asserts a mandatory requirement for disclosure, while the second implies that disclosure is conditional. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different sections (the first and second sentences) now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party does not acquire (by license or otherwise, whether express or implied) any intellectual property rights or other rights under this Agreement or any disclosure hereunder, except the limited right to use such Proprietary Information in accordance with the express provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party does acquire (by license or otherwise, whether express or implied) any intellectual property rights or other rights under this Agreement or any disclosure hereunder, including the right to use such Proprietary Information in accordance with the express provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the 'Receiving Party does not acquire' any intellectual property rights. The modified text states that the 'Receiving Party does acquire' those rights. This in-text contradicts with Section 5, sentence two 'Receiving Party does not acquire' any other rights under the agreement, which is contradicting the modified section that the Receiving Party does acquire other rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OWNERSHIP. All Proprietary Information (including without limitation, all copies, extracts and portions thereof) is and shall remain the sole property of Disclosing Party, provided , that all Derived Information shall be the sole property of Receiving Party. Receiving Party does acquire (by license or otherwise, whether express or implied) any intellectual property rights or other rights under this Agreement or any disclosure hereunder, including the right to use such Proprietary Information in accordance with the express provisions of this Agreement. All rights relating to the Proprietary Information that are not expressly granted hereunder to Interested Party are reserved and retained by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the 'Receiving Party does not acquire' any intellectual property rights, while the changed text states that the 'Receiving Party does acquire' those rights. The contradicted text, which contains the changed text, now has a contradiction. The second sentence of the contradicted text, reads 'Receiving Party does acquire (by license or otherwise, whether express or implied) any intellectual property rights or other rights under this Agreement or any disclosure hereunder, including the right to use such Proprietary Information in accordance with the express provisions of this Agreement.' contradicts with the sentence 'All Proprietary Information (including without limitation, all copies, extracts and portions thereof) is and shall remain the sole property of Disclosing Party, provided , that all Derived Information shall be the sole property of Receiving Party.' since the first sentence states that all Proprietary information is the property of the disclosing party while the second sentence states that the receiving party acquires those rights.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise provided herein, the obligations of this Agreement, including the restrictions on disclosure and use, shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date; provided that Sections 2.5, 2.6, and 2.7 and Articles 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise provided herein, the obligations of this Agreement, including the restrictions on disclosure and use, shall terminate immediately upon the Effective Date; provided that Sections 2.5, 2.6, and 2.7 and Articles 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement terminates on the second anniversary. The modified text states it terminates immediately upon the Effective Date. This in-text contradicts the purpose of the agreement itself. Other sections such as the AMENDMENT NO. 1 TO NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT which amends by replacing the second anniversary for the fourth anniversary creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AMENDMENT NO. 1 TO NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Amendment No. 1 to Non-Disclosure Agreement (this “ Amendment ”) dated as of May , 2010 (the “ Amendment Date ”), is entered into between Cogent, Inc., a Delaware corporation (“ Company ” or “ Disclosing Party ”) and 3M Company, a Delaware corporation (“ Interested Party ” or “ Receiving Party ”) (collectively the “ Parties ”). WHEREAS, the Parties previously entered into a Non-Disclosure Agreement dated as of July 31, 2008 (the “ Non-Disclosure Agreement ”); WHEREAS, the Parties wish to amend the Non-Disclosure Agreement in certain respects on the terms and conditions set forth herein. NOW THEREFORE, the Parties hereby agree as follows: 1. Section 3.1 of the Non-Disclosure Agreement is hereby amended by replacing “one year anniversary of the Effective Date” in the second line of such section with “three year anniversary of the Effective Date.” 2. Section 6 of the Non-Disclosure Agreement is hereby amended by replacing “second anniversary of the Effective Date” in the second line of such section with “fourth anniversary of the Effective Date.” 3. This Amendment shall be effective for all purposes as of the Amendment Date. The terms and conditions set forth in this Amendment are in addition to the terms of the Non-Disclosure Agreement which, except as amended in this Amendment, shall continue to remain in full force and effect in accordance with its terms. Any capitalized terms not otherwise defined herein shall have the meanings given such terms in the Non-Disclosure Agreement. 4. This Amendment may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be an original and together shall be deemed to be one and the same document. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Amendment to be executed by their respective duly authorized representatives effective as of the Amendment Date. 3M Company Cogent, Inc. By: /s/ David G. Fellner By: /s/ Paul Kim Name: David G. Fellner Name: Paul Kim Title: Manager, Corporate Development Title: Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement to terminate immediately upon the Effective Date, while the contradicted text (Amendment No. 1) explicitly amends the original agreement to extend specific sections (Section 6) to the 'fourth anniversary of the Effective Date.' This direct conflict in the termination timeline creates a strong in-text contradiction because the amendment assumes the agreement is still in effect after the immediate termination introduced by the changed text, thus breaking the contract's internal consistency and enforceability.
